rulings.Case Title: C.I. ENERGIA SOLAR S.A., INC., VS. BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC., ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff C.I. Energia Solar S.A. And E.S. Windows' Person Most Knowledgable And Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: October 10, 2019 Line 1, DEFENDANT BAGATELOS GLASS SYSTEMS, INC.'S, Motion To Compel The Deposition Of Plaintiff C.I. Energia Solar S.A. And E.S. Windows' Person Most Knowledgable And Production Of Documents Pro Tem Judge David McDonald, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to December 6, 2019 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept 301 by agreement of the Court and counsel. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN MANN ET AL VS. AJLOUN ENTERPRISES LLC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Ajloun Enterprises Llc And Muath Zghoul'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Written Discovery Responses And Documents
rulings.Rulings: October 10, 2019 Line 2, Defendants Ajloun Enterprises Llc And Muath Zghoul'S Motion To Compel Plaintiffs Further Written Discovery Responses And Documents The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion granted in part. The Court finds that the motion was timely filed, albeit without a Separate Statement. When Defendants later filed the Separate Statement, Plaintiff asked that the motion be re-noticed. Plaintiff now argues that the re-noticed motion is untimely. The Court finds this argument unpersuasive as, after all, it was Plaintiff that asked that the motion be re-noticed. The Court has reviewed the various discovery requests and grants the motion as to Form Interrogatory Nos. 9.1, 10.1, 10.2, 10.3, 12.4; Special Interrogatory Nos. 2, 5, 6, 7, 13, 15, 22, 24, 27, 28, RFPD Nos. 1-4, 7-10. The motion is denied as to Form Interrogatory No. 9.2, 11.1, Special Interrogatory Nos. 1, 4, 26 and 31. Supplemental responses to be provided by November 1, 2019. Because of Defendants' error in not filing a timely Separate Statement, the request for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to davididaho14@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. (jpt/302).

rulings.Case Title: TAELOR BOURGEOIS VS. LIZA NAVARRO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 10, 2019, Line 3, DEFENDANT LIZA NAVARRO's MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Defendant Navarro's motion for summary judgment is denied. There is a triable issue of material fact regarding whether defendant knew or had reason to know of Mowgli's vicious propensities. (See Priebe v. Nelson (2006) 39 Cal.4th 1112, 1115-1116 ["If [defendant] knew or should have known of his dog's vicious propensities and failed to inform [plaintiff] of such facts, he could be found to have exposed [plaintiff kennel worker] to an unknown risk and thereby be held strictly liable at common law for her injuries. [Citations.] Under such circumstances, the defense of primary assumption of risk would not bar [plaintiff's] claim since she could not be found to have assumed a risk of which she was unaware."]; Plaintiff's Ex. 5 (Bhakri Depo., 20:8-24; 21:23-22:15; 24:10-12); Plaintiff's Ex. 7 (Grewal Depo., 18:8-19; 20:1-10); Wine Dec., 1-9.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: JOHANNA WELTY VS. CRAIG W. MORRISON
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 10, 2019, Line 4, DEFENDANT CRAIG MORRISON's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT. Defendant Craig Morrison's demurrer to Plaintiff Johanna Welty's second amended complaint is sustained without leave to amend. This action for professional negligence was originally filed on May 31, 2017. A first amended complaint was filed on July 3, 2018. By order filed November 5, 2018, the Court sustained Defendant's unopposed demurrer to that complaint, which the Court found to be uncertain, ambiguous and unintelligible, and granted Plaintiff 10 days leave to amend. Plaintiff did not do so. By order filed November 29, 2018, the court granted Plaintiff 60 days leave to file a second amended complaint, due January 31, 2019. The order directed Plaintiff that the complaint was to be 20 pages or less (not including exhibits) and each legal theory was to be separately labeled. Plaintiff again did not comply with that order, and the Court granted Defendant's motion to dismiss the action pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure § 581(f)(2). By order dated May 30, 2019, however, the Court set aside that order and directed Plaintiff to file her second amended complaint on May 31, 2019. The Court's full tentative ruling is sent by email to the parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling adversely affects, in whole or in part, any party who is not represented by an attorney, then any other party (whether represented by an attorney or not) may submit on the tentative ruling without appearing for the hearing only if the adversely affected self-represented party has stated in writing or orally that she will not appear at the hearing. Counsel for defendant is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE WALKER ET AL VS. ANI PRIVATE SECURITY AND PATROL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Cahill Contractors, Inc., 923 Folsom Acquisition, Llc, Pacific Structures, Inc. Motion For Summary Judgment Notice Of Defendants Cahill Contractors, Inc.S; 923 Folsom Acquisition, Llcs; Align Real Estate, Llcs; And Pacific Structures, Incs Motion For Summary Judgment (For Tentative Ruling Only)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 10, 2019, Line 5, Defendant Cahill Contractors, Inc., 923 Folsom Acquisition, Llc, Pacific Structures, Inc.'s Motion For Summary Judgment. (part 2 of 2) (For Tentative Ruling Only). The evidence establishes that Ms. Cauich's attack on decedent was unpremeditated and plaintiff presents no proof that defendant had sufficient warning to prevent the attack. (Defendants' UMF 7.) The attack erupted within a matter of 20-30 seconds. (Plaintiff's Ex. 21, (Doyle Trial Testimony, 17:13-20.).) Plaintiff notes that the area around the worksite was riddled with drug paraphernalia, human feces, and an endemic transient population. (Davis Dec., Ex. 5 (Mooring Dec., 24).) Sadly, that has become all too common throughout a large part of San Francisco. It is unclear how such activity provided notice to defendant to intervene and to prevent the unpremeditated stabbing of decedent. "[W]here do we draw the line? How many guards are enough? Ten? Twenty? Two hundred? ... To characterize a landowner's failure to deter the wanton, mindless acts of violence of a third person as the 'cause' of the victim's injuries is (on these facts) to make the landowner the insurer of the absolute safety of everyone who enters the premises." (Nola M. v. University of Southern California (1993) 16 Cal.App.4th 421, 437.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for defendants is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: LESLIE WALKER ET AL VS. ANI PRIVATE SECURITY AND PATROL, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT Notice Of Defendants Cahill Contractors, Inc.S; 923 Folsom Acquisition, Llcs; Align Real Estate, Llcs; And Pacific Structures, Incs Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 10, 2019, Line 5, DEFENDANT CAHILL CONTRACTORS, INC., 923 FOLSOM ACQUISITION, LLC, PACIFIC STRUCTURES, INC.'s MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. (part 1 of 2) Defendants' Cahill Contractors, Inc., 923 Folsom Acquisition, LLC, Align Real Estate, LLC and Pacific Structures, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Plaintiff merely demonstrates "abstract negligence" and cannot establish causation as a matter of law. "In cases in which the specific conduct of third parties is brought to the attention of a defendant property owner sufficiently in advance of the injury to give the defendant an opportunity to act to prevent the injury, the causal connection between failure to act and the injury is patent…We are, however, unaware of any case in which a judgment against the property owner has been affirmed solely on the basis of a failure to provide an adequate deterrence to criminal conduct in general." (Noble v. L.A. Dodgers (1985) 168 Cal.App.3d 912, 916 [reversing verdict for plaintiff assaulted by drunken fans in baseball stadium parking lot where plaintiffs failed to prove any causal connection between team's alleged negligence in failing to provide greater security and plaintiff's injury]; see also Saelzer v. Advanced Group 400 (2001) 25 Cal.4th 763, 772-781 [affirming summary judgment for defendants where plaintiff's evidence was insufficient to show that defendants' asserted breach of duty in failing to provide additional security precautions at apartment building would have prevented her injuries caused by criminal assault of unknown assailants]; Rinehart v. Boys & Girls Club of Chula Vista (2005) 133 Cal.App.4th 419, 435 [affirming summary judgment for boys and girls club where plaintiff was injured when nonmember of club threw rock onto club playground; evidence merely showed speculative possibility that additional playground supervisors or repairs to fence on hill above playground might have deterred rock attack from hilltop].) In order to show actual causation, a plaintiff must present evidence that "discloses an actual and substantial causal link between the criminal assault and the defendant's negligence." (Saelzer v. Advanced Group 400, 25 Cal.4th at 779.) In this case, plaintiff fails to show that the existence of a security officer or employment of the "buddy system" would have prevented the attack. (Bowers Dec., 8-16.) "Put another way, she is unable to prove it was 'more probable than not' that additional security precautions would have prevented the attack." (Saelzler v. Advanced Group 400, 25 Cal.4th at 776; see id. at 681 ["speculative possibility that additional daytime security guards and/or functioning security gates might have prevented the assault" was insufficient to create a triable issue].) (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: RINA GONZALEZ VS. UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 10, 2019, Line 8, DEFENDANT ABM INDUSTRIES, INC.'s DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT.(part 1 of 2) Defendant ABM Industries, Inc.'s demurrer to Plaintiffs Rina Gonzalez and Gustavo Donis's second amended complaint is overruled in part and sustained in part. The demurrers to the first, second, fourth, fifth and seventh causes of action are overruled. The demurrer to the third cause of action is sustained with 20 days leave to amend. The demurrer as to the first cause of action for negligent hiring, training, supervision, and/or retention is overruled. Liberally construing the complaint, Plaintiffs plead sufficient facts to establish a claim. (See Code Civ. Proc., § 452; 2d Am. Compl. 10, 11, 15, 26-27.) Whether an employee/employer relationship existed between Defendant ABM and Doe 1 at the time of the alleged accident is presumptively within the knowledge of Defendant ABM. (See Chen v. Berenjian (2019) 33 Cal.App.5th 811, 822 [a demurrer for uncertainty should be overruled where the facts alleged in the complaint are presumptively within the knowledge of the demurring party or ascertainable by invoking discovery procedures].) The demurrer as to the second cause of action for negligence is overruled. Plaintiffs plead sufficient facts to establish a claim. (2d. Am. Compl. 17-18, 21-24.) Negligent hiring is a form of direct liability. (Flores v. Autozone West, Inc. (2008) 161 Cal.App.4th 373, 384.) Therefore, the type of duty alleged in the second cause of action is not duplicative or redundant. (Cf., e.g. Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200, 1205 ["It is well established in California that although a store owner is not an insurer of the safety of its patrons, the owner does owe them a duty to exercise reasonable care in keeping the premises reasonably safe."].) The demurrer as to the third cause of action for negligence per se is sustained. The negligence per se doctrine creates a presumption of negligence that affects the burden of proof. (Evid. Code, § 669 subd. (a)(1)-(3).) The Court recognizes negligence per se is not a cause of action; however, Plaintiffs may plead different theories of liability. (See Mendoza v. Continental Sales Co. (2006) 140 Cal.App.4th 1395, 1402.) The demurrer to this cause of action is sustained because Plaintiffs' second amended complaint fails to allege any specific law or regulation Defendant ABM allegedly violated. (See Ramirez v. Nelson (2008) 44 Cal.4th 908, 917-918.) Plaintiffs have 20 days leave to amend, if they can do so in good faith, to allege that Defendant's conduct violated such a specific statute or regulation. The demurrer as to the fourth cause of action for assault and battery and the fifth cause of action for IIED are overruled. Both causes of action are against Defendant Doe 1, not Defendant ABM. The demurrer as to the seventh cause of action for negligent infliction of emotional distress is overruled. Plaintiffs sufficiently allege the elements of negligent infliction. (See Thing v. La Chusa (1989) 48 Cal.3d 644, 647.); 2d Am. Compl. 56-58.) Defendant argues the seventh cause of action is uncertain and unclear as to who is asserting the claim. However, the cause of action contains enough facts to ascertain Plaintiff Donis is asserting the claim against Defendants. (See Williams v. Beechnut Nutrition Corp. (1986) 1985 Cal.App.3d 135, 139 [if the complaint contains enough facts to apprise the defendant of the issues, failure to label each cause of action is not a ground for demurrer].) (end of part 1 of 2) =(302/EPS)
